title: Iran targets military bases as US launches wave of strikes
description: Explosions were heard across Iran overnight, shortly after neighbouring Gulf states began to report attacks.

title: Argentina face action over Falklands banner
description: Argentina face the prospect of disciplinary action from Fifa after their players celebrate the World Cup semi-final win against England with a banner in support of their country's claims to the Falkland Islands.

title: Families of 43 victims await verdict in Genoa bridge disaster
description: A large section of the giant Morandi motorway bridge crumbled and collapsed on to the railway tracks below.

title: US military to start screening for testosterone deficiency, Hegseth says
description: Hegseth says he is authorising hormone screening to ensure US troops "operate at your absolute best".

title: Actor Sam Neill died from pneumonia, agent confirms
description: Neill died in Sydney on Monday, with his family saying his death was 'sudden and unexpected'.

title: More people around the world now favour China over the US, Pew study suggests
description: There is more confidence in Xi Jinping than Donald Trump, the US think tank's survey indicates.

title: New monkey species with orange lips found 'hiding' in DR Congo forest
description: Scientists describe "amazing feeling" to look into the face of an animal that so few people knew existed.

title: French MPs approve assisted dying law with strict rules after years of argument
description: The bill would allow assisted dying for terminally ill adults who meet strict criteria.

title: Russian attacks kill 14 as Ukraine hits Black Sea oil tankers
description: Civilian casualties are reported in a number of Ukrainian regions, while Kyiv hits 20 Russian vessels in the Black Sea.

title: Special police team to investigate killing of an anti-migrant leader in South Africa
description: The March and March provincial leader died of his injuries in hospital several days after being shot.

title: World News in Brief: Schools closed in West Bank, AI in healthcare, Indigenous rights
description: Access to education continues to worsen in the West Bank, the United Nations reported on Wednesday.

title: Gulf crisis: Guterres calls for de-escalation, warns against return to full-on war
description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres voiced deep concern on Wednesday over the continuing military escalation in the Middle East amid ongoing strikes by the United States and Iran over control of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Security Council LIVE: International Criminal Court briefs on Darfur
description: On Wednesday, the Security Council heard an update on the International Criminal Court's work in Sudan's Darfur region, referred to the Court back in 2005. Focus has recently turned to atrocities in the ongoing war between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF). The briefing was held as the ICC faces mounting pressure: this week, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio launched a campaign to “dismantle” the Court, which prosecutes individuals for genocide, war crimes, crimes against humanity and the crime of aggression. Follow full live coverage below.

title: New guidance offers hope for reducing the global toll of dementia
description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has issued updated global guidelines aimed at reducing the risk of cognitive decline and dementia, saying that up to 45 per cent of cases may be preventable or delayed by addressing modifiable risk factors throughout life.

title: Colombia: Pockets of insecurity challenge ‘vibrant democracy’, Security Council told
description: As Colombia awaits the inauguration of its newly elected president, pockets of insecurity remain a challenge for a nation that has overcome decades of conflict, the head of the UN verification team there told the Security Council on Wednesday.

title: Looted gold and gum arabic are bankrolling Sudan's war, UN warns
description: Alongside gold, the little-known Sudanese commodity gum arabic – used in soft drinks, food, cosmetics and pharmaceuticals – continues to fuel the war.

title: Childhood vaccination rate increases slightly, but millions remain unprotected
description: Global childhood immunization programmes continued to recover in 2025, but conflict, poverty and growing vaccine hesitancy are still leaving millions vulnerable to preventable diseases, according to new UN data released on Wednesday.

title: Skills for an uncertain future: How youth can navigate a changing job market
description: Choosing a path for the future has never been easy, but for young people today, such rapid technological change as artificial intelligence (AI) is making it increasingly difficult to predict which skills will remain relevant in the years ahead.

title: World Cup football’s other goal: Helping young people talk about mental health
description: When Dr. Sahira Al Nahari founded Shifā Art, a Saudi Arabia-based organization using art to create conversations around mental health, she noticed that men attending her therapeutic art workshops often felt uncomfortable opening up about their feelings.

title: World News in Brief: Gaza aid challenges persist, renewed push for clean energy, Sudan cholera update
description: Humanitarians in the Gaza Strip continue to face significant challenges in reaching communities located near the so-called “Yellow Line” in northern Rafah, the UN aid coordination office OCHA said on Tuesday.

title: Gulf crisis: Guterres calls for de-escalation, warns against return to full-on war
description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres voiced deep concern on Wednesday over the continuing military escalation in the Middle East amid ongoing strikes by the United States and Iran over control of the Strait of Hormuz.

description: As Colombia awaits the inauguration of its newly elected president, pockets of insecurity remain a challenge for a nation that has overcome decades of conflict, the head of the UN verification team there told the Security Council on Wednesday.

title: Looted gold and gum arabic are bankrolling Sudan's war, UN warns
description: Alongside gold, the little-known Sudanese commodity gum arabic – used in soft drinks, food, cosmetics and pharmaceuticals – continues to fuel the war.

title: West Africa and the Sahel: Terrorism is changing its face
description: Jihadist groups are no longer content with launching attacks in West Africa and the Sahel: they administer territories, control trade routes, exploit new technologies and are gradually pushing their influence all the way to the Gulf of Guinea.

title: ‘Cycle of escalation must end’: UN condemns deadly Strait of Hormuz attacks
description: The UN maritime agency, IMO, condemned overnight attacks on shipping in and around the Strait of Hormuz that killed at least two seafarers as fresh strikes were reported early Tuesday in the escalating US-Iran war.

title: ‘This is a fire’: DRC Ebola outbreak is fastest-growing ever, warns WHO
description: Infections of the Bundibugyo species of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) have reached record highs and a majority of new cases are coming from “unknown chains of transmission”, the UN World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Tuesday.

title: World News in Brief: Aid deliveries to Gaza restricted, UN prepares El Nino response, El Salvador eliminates disease
description: The UN World Food Programme (WFP) is calling for greater humanitarian access to Gaza, warning that restrictions on aid deliveries, ongoing violence and funding shortages are severely limiting its ability to reach people in need.

title: Strait of Hormuz: UN agencies call for diplomacy to end attacks
description: As both Washington and Tehran claim to control the critical commercial shipping route through the narrow Strait of Hormuz, UN agencies on Monday are calling for de-escalation amid the recent spike in strikes in the region related to the US-Iran war.

title: Security Council LIVE: Top UN officials call for de-escalation in Yemen
description: As the UN chief’s Special Envoy for Yemen, Hans Grundberg, calls for swift de-escalation following reports of Saudi Arabian airstrikes and Iranian aircraft landing in the country, the Security Council’s emergency meeting at 3pm (local time) on Monday heard briefings from top officials on the latest humanitarian and political situation.

title: On Our Radar
description: On Our Radar eschelhaas Fri, 07/03/2026 - 19:07 Latest Updates Africa Asia-Pacific Europe Latin America & Caribbean Middle East & North Africa United States Global Issues & Institutions My Reading List Special Coverage / Global 03 July 2026 20+ minutes On Our Radar On Our Radar scans conflicts and crises around the globe every week and features some of the hotspots Crisis Group’s analysts are closely watching. Whether an under-reported trend or a headline-grabbing development, our field experts explain why it matters or what should be done. Share BlueSky Email Facebook Linkedin Twitter Whatsapp Save Print 3 July 2026 AFGHANISTAN - PAKISTAN Tensions between Afghanistan and Pakistan flared again this week. On Sunday, Pakistani forces once again carried out strikes across the border in Afghanistan, saying they killed 29 militants. Islamabad said the strikes were retaliation for recent attacks inside Pakistan, including a Karachi assault that killed three security personnel. According to the UN, at least 28 civilians were killed and another 49 injured in the strikes. Pakistan accuses the Afghan Taliban of sheltering anti-Pakistan militants, which Taliban officials deny. On Wednesday, Afghanistan said it carried out strikes inside Pakistan, targeting ISIS and other armed opposition groups. Islamabad denies Taliban accusations of harbouring such groups and noted that it had intercepted four drones launched from Afghan territory. Crisis Group expert Ibraheem Bahiss says outside mediators should push the sides toward practical border and security arrangements before the cycle of strikes and counterstrikes escalates further. NIGERIA Violence is again escalating in north-western Nigeria. Long plagued by bandits who engage in mass kidnappings for ransom, pillage and extortion, the region is also now seeing increased jihadist activity. Together, bandit and jihadist attacks killed over 150 people in Katsina, Kebbi, Sokoto and Zamfara states in June, a notable uptick from previous months. On 28 May, Islamic State Sahel Province claimed what appears to be its first reported attacks in the region, in which eighteen soldiers and one police officer were killed in Kebbi and Sokoto states. Amid this escalation, on 20 June prominent bandit leader Bello Turji claimed responsibility for deadly attacks on troops and declared his group ready for either peace talks or prolonged war with the government. If the bandit and jihadist threats are not reined in, says Crisis Group expert Nnamdi Obasi, north-western Nigeria could become part of the wider belt of instability in the Sahel. SUDAN Fighting centred on El Obeid, North Kordofan’s state capital, has intensified in recent weeks as drone strikes increase and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) mobilise around the city, raising fears of encirclement and blockade. UN relief chief Tom Fletcher warned Tuesday that El Obeid risks becoming “another El Fasher”, referring to North Darfur’s capital, which endured a prolonged siege before falling to the RSF last October and suffering mass atrocities. Crisis Group expert Shewit Woldemichael says a siege of El Obeid would deepen the humanitarian crisis facing civilians, many of whom have already fled to the city from fighting elsewhere. Meanwhile, the increased use of drones against El Obeid could allow for sustained attacks through the now-beginning rainy season, when ground fighting has typically slowed. 26 June 2026 BOLIVIA President Rodrigo Paz declared a state of exception starting Saturday after more than 50 days of protests triggered by a land reform law and planned austerity measures. Authorities say at least 37 people have been injured in clashes between demonstrators and police. The decree bans the protesters’ roadblocks that have paralysed commercial traffic, causing shortages of basic goods in the administrative capital La Paz, and (temporarily) allows the armed forces to assist police in reopening key routes. It does not, however, suspend fundamental rights, as is often the case with states of exception in Latin America. Before making the declaration, Paz had signed an agreement to “pacify” the country with the biggest trade union, the Bolivian Workers’ Central, which withdrew from the streets. Most of the roadblocks have vanished since the measure took effect. But Crisis Group expert Glaeldys González Calanche says tensions remain high. The unrest could erupt anew if the government fails to deliver on socio-economic commitments it made to the union or if other groups involved in the protests, including campesinos and Indigenous people, decide to resume the pressure. COLOMBIA Right-wing Abelardo de la Espriella won Sunday’s presidential runoff by the thinnest margin in Colombian history, defeating Iván Cepeda, who had campaigned on continuing leftist President Gustavo Petro’s social reform agenda. De la Espriella styles himself as an outsider. Boasting of his ties to U.S. President Donald Trump, who endorsed him before the vo

title: Hormuz 1 July 2026 #1
description: Hormuz 1 July 2026 #1 lalasor Fri, 07/03/2026 - 16:41 Strait of Hormuz Fri, 07/03/2026 - 12:00 1 July 2026 U.S. Central Command announced that it had led a security dialogue attended by senior military officials from a dozen regional states who “discussed the current regional security environment and opportunities for enhancing defence collaboration across the region”. It noted that “leaders underscored their shared commitment to the free flow of commerce through the Strait of Hormuz”. Iran’s foreign minister countered that “p eace in our region can only be sustained when comprehensive and inclusive, with no outside interference” .

title: Oracle E-Business Suite Improper Privilege Management Vulnerability
description: Oracle E-Business Suite contains an improper privilege management vulnerability that allows an unauthenticated attacker with network access via HTTP to compromise Oracle Payments. Successful attacks of this vulnerability can result in takeover of Oracle Payments.

title: KNX Association KNX Protocol Connection Authorization Option 1 Overly Restrictive Account Lockout Mechanism Vulnerability
description: KNX Association KNX Protocol Connection Authorization Option 1 contains an overly restrictive account lockout mechanism vulnerability that could allow an attacker to purge all devices without additional security options enabled and set a BCU key to lock the device.

title: Microsoft Active Directory Federation Services Insufficient Granularity of Access Control Vulnerability
description: Microsoft Active Directory Federation Services contains an insufficient granularity of access control vulnerability that allows an authorized attacker to elevate privileges locally.

title: Microsoft SharePoint Server Missing Authentication for Critical Function Vulnerability
description: Microsoft SharePoint contains a missing authentication for critical function vulnerability that allows an unauthorized attacker to elevate privileges over a network.

title: SonicWall SMA1000 Appliances Server-Side Request Forgery Vulnerability
description: SonicWall SMA1000 Appliances contain a server-side request forgery vulnerability that could allow a remote unauthenticated attacker to potentially cause the appliance to make requests to unintended location.

title: SonicWall SMA1000 Appliances Code Injection Vulnerability
description: SonicWall SMA1000 Appliances contain a code injection vulnerability which in specific conditions could potentially enable a remote authenticated attacker as administrator to execute arbitrary OS commands.

title: Cisco IOS Cross-Site Request Forgery Vulnerability
description: Cisco IOS 12.4 contains multiple cross-site forgery vulnerabilities that allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via (1) a certain "show privilege" command to the /level/15/exec/- URI, and (2) a certain "alias exec" command to the /level/15/exec/-/configure/http URI.

title: Balbooa Forms Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type Vulnerability
description: Balbooa Forms contains an unrestricted upload of file with dangerous type vulnerability that allows an unauthenticated arbitrary file upload which could allow uploading of executable files leading to full RCE.

title: iCagenda Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type Vulnerability
description: iCagenda contains an unrestricted upload of file with dangerous type vulnerability that allows the upload of arbitrary files in the file attachment feature, ultimately resulting in PHP code upload and execution.

title: JoomShaper SP Page Builder Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type Vulnerability
description: JoomShaper SP Page Builder contains an unrestricted upload of file with dangerous type vulnerability that allows unauthenticated users to upload arbitrary files, ultimately resulting in the upload and execution of PHP code.

title: Langflow Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key Vulnerability
description: Langflow contains an authorization bypass through user-controlled key vulnerability which allows an authenticated attacker to execute any flow belonging to another user by specifying the victim's flow ID in the request.

title: Joomlack Page Builder Improper Access Control Vulnerability
description: Joomlack Page Builder contains an improper access control vulnerability that could allow for remote code execution via unauthenticated arbitrary file upload.

title: Adobe ColdFusion Path Traversal Vulnerability
description: Adobe ColdFusion contains a path traversal vulnerability that could lead to arbitrary code execution in the context of the current user.

title: Microsoft SharePoint Server Deserialization of Untrusted Data Vulnerability
description: Microsoft SharePoint Server contains a deserialization of untrusted data vulnerability which allows an authorized attacker to execute code over a network.

title: SimpleHelp Authentication Bypass Vulnerability
description: SimpleHelp contains an authentication bypass vulnerability in the OIDC authentication flow. When OIDC authentication is configured, identity tokens submitted during login are accepted without verifying their cryptographic signature. In a vulnerable configuration, a remote, unauthenticated attacker can submit a forged token containing arbitrary identity claims to obtain a fully authenticated technician session. In some configurations, this may also allow bypass of multi-factor authentication.

title: PTC Windchill and FlexPLM Improper Input Validation Vulnerability
description: PTC Windchill and FlexPLM contains an improper input validation vulnerability allowing an unauthenticated, remote attacker to execute arbitrary code by sending a malicious request to the network.

title: Cisco Unified Communications Manager Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) Vulnerability
description: Cisco Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) and Cisco Unified Communications Manager Session Management Edition (Unified CM SME) contain a server-side request forgery (SSRF) Vulnerability that could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to write files to the underlying operating system that could be used later to elevate to root.

title: Lantronix EDS5000 Code Injection Vulnerability
description: Lantronix EDS5000 contains a code injection vulnerability that could allow attackers to inject arbitrary OS commands into the username parameter. Injected commands are executed with root privileges.

title: Ubiquiti UniFi OS Improper Input Validation Vulnerability
description: Ubiquiti UniFi OS contains an improper input validation vulnerability which could allow a malicious actor with access to the network to conduct command injection.

title: Ubiquiti UniFi OS Path Traversal Vulnerability
description: Ubiquiti UniFi OS contains a path traversal vulnerability which could allow a malicious actor with access to the network to access files on the underlying system that could be manipulated to access an underlying account.

title: Ubiquiti UniFi OS Improper Access Control Vulnerability
description: Ubiquiti UniFi OS contains an improper access control vulnerability which could allow a malicious actor with access to the network to make unauthorized changes to the system.

title: Splunk Enterprise Missing Authentication for Critical Function Vulnerability
description: Splunk Enterprise contains a missing authentication for critical function vulnerability which could allow an unauthenticated user to create or truncate arbitrary files through a PostgreSQL sidecar service endpoint.

title: Widget Factory Joomla Content Editor Improper Access Control Vulnerability
description: Widget Factory Joomla Content Editor contains an improper access control vulnerability which could allow for upload and execution of PHP code via the creation of new editor profiles for unauthenticated users.

title: LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin UNIX Symbolic Link (Symlink) Following Vulnerability
description: LiteSpeed cPanel plugin contains a UNIX symbolic link (Symlink) following vulnerability that could allow a user with FTP or web shell access on a shared hosting server running CloudLinux/CageFS.

title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager Directory or Path Traversal Vulnerability
description: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager contains a directory or path traversal vulnerability that could allow an authenticated, remote attacker to create a file or overwrite any file on the filesystem of an affected system.

title: Eleven people die in fire at Algerian foster care home
description: Along with the fatalities, 19 people suffered injuries, the authorities say.

title: More people around the world now favour China over the US, Pew study suggests
description: There is more confidence in Xi Jinping than Donald Trump, the US think tank's survey indicates.

title: Russian attacks kill 14 as Ukraine hits Black Sea oil tankers
description: Civilian casualties are reported in a number of Ukrainian regions, while Kyiv hits 20 Russian vessels in the Black Sea.

title: Gulf crisis: Guterres calls for de-escalation, warns against return to full-on war
description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres voiced deep concern on Wednesday over the continuing military escalation in the Middle East amid ongoing strikes by the United States and Iran over control of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: World News in Brief: Gaza aid challenges persist, renewed push for clean energy, Sudan cholera update
description: Humanitarians in the Gaza Strip continue to face significant challenges in reaching communities located near the so-called “Yellow Line” in northern Rafah, the UN aid coordination office OCHA said on Tuesday.

title: On Our Radar
description: On Our Radar eschelhaas Fri, 07/03/2026 - 19:07 Latest Updates Africa Asia-Pacific Europe Latin America & Caribbean Middle East & North Africa United States Global Issues & Institutions My Reading List Special Coverage / Global 03 July 2026 20+ minutes On Our Radar On Our Radar scans conflicts and crises around the globe every week and features some of the hotspots Crisis Group’s analysts are closely watching. Whether an under-reported trend or a headline-grabbing development, our field experts explain why it matters or what should be done. Share BlueSky Email Facebook Linkedin Twitter Whatsapp Save Print 3 July 2026 AFGHANISTAN - PAKISTAN Tensions between Afghanistan and Pakistan flared again this week. On Sunday, Pakistani forces once again carried out strikes across the border in Afghanistan, saying they killed 29 militants. Islamabad said the strikes were retaliation for recent attacks inside Pakistan, including a Karachi assault that killed three security personnel. According to the UN, at least 28 civilians were killed and another 49 injured in the strikes. Pakistan accuses the Afghan Taliban of sheltering anti-Pakistan militants, which Taliban officials deny. On Wednesday, Afghanistan said it carried out strikes inside Pakistan, targeting ISIS and other armed opposition groups. Islamabad denies Taliban accusations of harbouring such groups and noted that it had intercepted four drones launched from Afghan territory. Crisis Group expert Ibraheem Bahiss says outside mediators should push the sides toward practical border and security arrangements before the cycle of strikes and counterstrikes escalates further. NIGERIA Violence is again escalating in north-western Nigeria. Long plagued by bandits who engage in mass kidnappings for ransom, pillage and extortion, the region is also now seeing increased jihadist activity. Together, bandit and jihadist attacks killed over 150 people in Katsina, Kebbi, Sokoto and Zamfara states in June, a notable uptick from previous months. On 28 May, Islamic State Sahel Province claimed what appears to be its first reported attacks in the region, in which eighteen soldiers and one police officer were killed in Kebbi and Sokoto states. Amid this escalation, on 20 June prominent bandit leader Bello Turji claimed responsibility for deadly attacks on troops and declared his group ready for either peace talks or prolonged war with the government. If the bandit and jihadist threats are not reined in, says Crisis Group expert Nnamdi Obasi, north-western Nigeria could become part of the wider belt of instability in the Sahel. SUDAN Fighting centred on El Obeid, North Kordofan’s state capital, has intensified in recent weeks as drone strikes increase and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) mobilise around the city, raising fears of encirclement and blockade. UN relief chief Tom Fletcher warned Tuesday that El Obeid risks becoming “another El Fasher”, referring to North Darfur’s capital, which endured a prolonged siege before falling to the RSF last October and suffering mass atrocities. Crisis Group expert Shewit Woldemichael says a siege of El Obeid would deepen the humanitarian crisis facing civilians, many of whom have already fled to the city from fighting elsewhere. Meanwhile, the increased use of drones against El Obeid could allow for sustained attacks through the now-beginning rainy season, when ground fighting has typically slowed. 26 June 2026 BOLIVIA President Rodrigo Paz declared a state of exception starting Saturday after more than 50 days of protests triggered by a land reform law and planned austerity measures. Authorities say at least 37 people have been injured in clashes between demonstrators and police. The decree bans the protesters’ roadblocks that have paralysed commercial traffic, causing shortages of basic goods in the administrative capital La Paz, and (temporarily) allows the armed forces to assist police in reopening key routes. It does not, however, suspend fundamental rights, as is often the case with states of exception in Latin America. Before making the declaration, Paz had signed an agreement to “pacify” the country with the biggest trade union, the Bolivian Workers’ Central, which withdrew from the streets. Most of the roadblocks have vanished since the measure took effect. But Crisis Group expert Glaeldys González Calanche says tensions remain high. The unrest could erupt anew if the government fails to deliver on socio-economic commitments it made to the union or if other groups involved in the protests, including campesinos and Indigenous people, decide to resume the pressure. COLOMBIA Right-wing Abelardo de la Espriella won Sunday’s presidential runoff by the thinnest margin in Colombian history, defeating Iván Cepeda, who had campaigned on continuing leftist President Gustavo Petro’s social reform agenda. De la Espriella styles himself as an outsider. Boasting of his ties to U.S. President Donald Trump, who endorsed him before the vo

title: Hormuz 1 July 2026 #1
description: Hormuz 1 July 2026 #1 lalasor Fri, 07/03/2026 - 16:41 Strait of Hormuz Fri, 07/03/2026 - 12:00 1 July 2026 U.S. Central Command announced that it had led a security dialogue attended by senior military officials from a dozen regional states who “discussed the current regional security environment and opportunities for enhancing defence collaboration across the region”. It noted that “leaders underscored their shared commitment to the free flow of commerce through the Strait of Hormuz”. Iran’s foreign minister countered that “p eace in our region can only be sustained when comprehensive and inclusive, with no outside interference” .

description: Israel 3 July 2026 #1 lalasor Fri, 07/03/2026 - 16:36 Israel Fri, 07/03/2026 - 12:00 3 July 2026 The IDF reported that “in response to Hizbollah’s repeated ceasefire violations: [approximately] ten Hizbollah infrastructure sites in southern Lebanon, which were used to advance attacks against IDF soldiers, were struck. Additionally, IDF soldiers identified and struck a Hizbollah terrorist cell transporting weapons in a truck near the security zone”.

title: Lebanon 3 July 2026 #1
description: Lebanon 3 July 2026 #1 lalasor Fri, 07/03/2026 - 16:35 Lebanon Fri, 07/03/2026 - 12:00 3 July 2026 The IDF reported that “in response to Hizbollah’s repeated ceasefire violations: [approximately] ten Hizbollah infrastructure sites in southern Lebanon, which were used to advance attacks against IDF soldiers, were struck. Additionally, IDF soldiers identified and struck a Hizbollah terrorist cell transporting weapons in a truck near the security zone”.

title: Tehran 3 July 2026 #1
description: Tehran 3 July 2026 #1 lalasor Fri, 07/03/2026 - 16:34 Tehran Fri, 07/03/2026 - 12:00 3 July 2026 Iranian media cited the speaker of parliament as stressing that Iran would “not allow any U.S. interference in the Strait of Hormuz”, positing too that “Israel is undoubtedly seeking to undermine the Iran–U.S. memorandum of understanding, but…the Islamic Republic’s deterrence capability in the region would prevent Israel from launching another war”.

title: Childhood vaccination rate increases slightly, but millions remain unprotected
description: Global childhood immunization programmes continued to recover in 2025, but conflict, poverty and growing vaccine hesitancy are still leaving millions vulnerable to preventable diseases, according to new UN data released on Wednesday.
